reviews.author: Therapy Hoshin
reviews.text: I went on a weekend, it was very crowded but Trung Nguyen Legend's staff handled it quickly, I didn't have to wait in line to buy tickets. Go down to see the beautiful scenery and the water is very cool. Food and drinks are available for sale. There is one thing I am not very satisfied with. When I bought 2 cups of coffee on the counter before going down to Dray Nur waterfall, the coffee was filled to the brim and transferred 80,000 VND to the Trung Nguyen system. Then when I got down to the waterfall, I continued to buy 2 cups of coffee, but at this time the price increased, I had to pay 100,000 VND and could only brew half a cup, and had to transfer the money to the employee's H'Chem…personal account.... I find it a bit unreasonable, you should buy coffee over the counter because the coffee is very delicious

reviews.author: Sandra Pfeiffer
reviews.text: The waterfall is nice. You can walk over from Dray Sap waterfall. There used to be a bridge which is gone now but if you have good balance and shoes you can hop over stones from one side to the other. It's quieter than Dray Sap and smaller. But also here there is rubbish everywhere. Nice to have but no must.

reviews.author: Sam Ng
reviews.text: I went on a bad day, the slope down bike parking has loose stones and earth. I fell and injured my thumb and knee. My thumb nail got ripped off, with lots of bleeding. Since they are collecting admission, they should ensure their facility is safe for visitors. Consolation is their staff applied first aid for me. It is dry season and there is not much water. The road in is under construction.

reviews.author: ThiThien Cross
reviews.text: It’s great experience! Beautiful waterfall we just visited 2 weeks ago!

reviews.author: Dmitriy Veremskiy
reviews.text: When we was waterfall was not so big, but it enough impressive for great foto. Dont forget do foto from left to Waterfall.

reviews.author: Duy Phong Trieu
reviews.text: Big waterfall. Going in mid year would be more spectacular as raining season. Strong flow

reviews.author: Kevin Tu
reviews.text: Better in rainy season but dry season ok too. Lots of other attractions around thou

reviews.author: RenOverSea
reviews.text: Nice double waterfall on the border between Dak Lak & Dak Nong Province. Easy to reach from Dak Mil on the busy QL14, or the rural road DT683/DT684, which is more scenic with less traffic but with partially challenging road surfaces.

reviews.author: Mikelis
reviews.text: The bridge from the east has been washed away. There is still the other bridge on west side. With good shoes you can walk on the rocks closer to the waterfall

reviews.author: Jean Tran
reviews.text: Beautiful creatures. Not recommend traveling here by bike because the trip is long with many big containers on the road. The area has restroom and restaurants, food and drinks (and ice-cream) are reasonable priced. Would recommend preparing bug spray and sunscreen.

reviews.author: Paul Jay
reviews.text: Was lucky to see it in Full flight. There are several waterfalls close together here.

reviews.author: Minh Le Nhat (Nexus)
reviews.text: The waterfall is really wide. I visit here around December which is a good visiting time. The water flow also might vary within the day. At night, it seems to be less water than during the day. The more water, the more powerful the waterfall look like. Anayway , the scene surrounding is very beautiful. There is a restarant here serving jungle food. The entry ticket is VND 30.000 for an adult.

reviews.author: Tien Tran
reviews.text: It’s beautiful and powerful! The waterfall itself is impressive, but the surrounding area is pretty small. It wasn’t crowded during my visit, which was lucky. Honestly, I’d avoid visiting on weekends or holidays since it could get cramped. Overall, worth seeing for the waterfall, just try to pick a quieter time.

reviews.author: Kien Thuy Pham
reviews.text: Wonderfully beautiful waterfall. The road to get here from the main road is pretty good for the most part. Some areas of unexpected potholes, so do not drive to fast. The waterfall park is well run. There is a place to buy food and drink and a souvenir shop. The waterfall is impressive, and the paths around the area are well cleared and easy to follow. You can bathe in the water, but bear in mind that you can get parasites, especially from stagnant water (it happened to me once, but not from this location). This is a great place to feel at one with nature, relax and even have a picnic. Loved ittttttttt hereeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeee.

reviews.author: Christina Pyzh
reviews.text: We visited this waterfall during the peak of the rainy season, so the water wasn’t as clear as in some photos. However, the recent typhoon left us with the most massive and powerful waterfall we’ve ever seen. Parking costs 10,000 VND, and the entrance fee to Dray Nur is 60,000 VND. We assumed this would cover the entire park, but the ticket booth didn’t inform us that there’s an additional fee of 80,000 VND to see Dray Sap. You only find out after walking for a while, which was a bit inconvenient. That aside, it’s a stunning spot for a picnic, and you can even bring your furry friend along.

reviews.author: garry killen
reviews.text: Quite a ride down a terrible condition road on the way there. I rode a different but longer route out which was better. Very tranquil. And the falls were quite quiet. I think after some heavy rain they may be more spectacular.

reviews.author: RaphTbm
reviews.text: Very unique place. Great waterfall. Lot of area to chill around. Don't forget to take a look at the back at the waterfall. Golden hours light is perfect for this place.

reviews.author: Jean-Francois Perigois
reviews.text: Not easy to find the place on your own when you ride a motorbike as there is no information about the site along the road. Best way is to follow the AH17 and check google map regulary !

reviews.author: Haim Kesselgross
reviews.text: When we were there it was extra impressive since it was the end of the rainy season and the water flow was amazing. As with all nature sights in vietnam and most of south east Asia the repulsive, unnecessary addition of all sorts of artificial stuff, such as cement birds an King Kongs, or flower arches, reduces the whole excitement .

reviews.author: Trung Thành Vũ
reviews.text: The Dray Nur waterfall is beautiful! It sprays water on peiple who go close to it . It is amazing !

reviews.author: Ho Ngoc Tin
reviews.text: We booked healing rafting tour from Trung Nguyen, including bike, trekking and boat! really beautiful waterfalls from Gia Long to Dray Nur! Emerald water, we can swim there! Lunch is normal food.

reviews.author: Huyen Tran
reviews.text: Beautiful waterfall! Water is clean and clear, it's like having a walk into the tropical forest. Should check it out! We entered this from Dray Sap waterfall. Ticket is 30k per person. There is a bridge connect between these 2 waterfalls. Make sure you visit both!

reviews.author: Jefferson Saunders
reviews.text: My friends and I hiked all over the place. We enjoyed it all. Go at a quieter time of the week. One tour guide asked us if the litter was too much. If the karaoke was rude and should smoking and alcohol be banned. We decided not to reply. Please pick up litter and respect others. This is a special place Please visit.

reviews.author: 武HEERA
reviews.text: We rent a motobike from Buon Ma Thuot center go to Dray nur waterfall took around 1 hour Beautiful waterfull very chill and relaxing I came here 2 times The 2nd time there are have some team buiding singing karaoke very fun and fancy but i don’t think it’s suitable for everyone specially who didn’t noisy. But you should come for a views this is wonderful nature creation Love this place

reviews.author: Nhuy Nguyen
reviews.text: It was Too crowded at national holiday. Tô many family gathers together to do BBQ. Entrance tickets is 30k đong/ person. The suspension bridge swang too much 🥹 so scary to pass it

reviews.author: Johnathan MacDonald
reviews.text: Visited just before the start of the wet season this fall and the ne t one a km up are worth the visit. The basalt columns that had fallen over on the side were fascinating.

reviews.author: boredguy42
reviews.text: One of Buon Ma Thuat's remarkable waterfalls. Entry tickets are affordable, there are tables for picnics and plenty of space for taking photos.

reviews.author: Trang Nguyen
reviews.text: LOVE THIS PLACE! It was so beutiful, the scenery was just unbelieveable -in other words, it was just out of this world. Highly recommend this place to both families and people who love outdoor activities because it will take your breath away. Words cannot describe how much I love this place. This place makes you feel so relaxed and calm and not to mention the food here is great. Super delicious, I really recommend the sticky rice on a bambo-tube, the second best thing on this adventure. This place is also very Instagram-friendly. You'll get amazing photos that will surely keep the likes flowing in! Your trip won't be complete without visiting this place, you'll thank me later I guarantee it.

reviews.author: Wence Rivera
reviews.text: Rain fell in the area for several days before we visited so the waterfall was flowing quite well. There's a spot near the back that allows you to shower under the waterfall. There are areas to have a picnic and little shop that sells food and souvenirs. This site is mainly visited by locals as the road to the waterfall has many potholes and slow going. But it is still worth a visit.

reviews.author: Hieu Nguyen
reviews.text: Not quite good. I was very disappointed with this attraction. There was still waterfall but it was not like the picture you saw on the Internet because the volume of water was reduced a lot by hydroelectric plants being built on upstream. There was not much service here. The only restaurant serving food was pretty old and poor. Locals brought beer, snacks and fruits, eat and litter right over there. The only thing I liked was free and nice coffee being served deep inside the place.
